read.image: loving someone you would not call back
read.take: The specific nausea of being tangled up with someone who hurt you and still takes up room in your day. She isn't asking for closure. She is naming the dizziness of missing a person she also can't stand. Anger and longing share the same sentence and refuse to resolve.
read.contradiction: She hates him for what he did and misses him anyway. Both stay true.

arc.label: Verse 1
arc.scene: She lists the damage in an almost conversational shrug, like recounting a bad trip to a friend.

arc.label: Chorus
arc.scene: The motion sickness hits. The melody lifts while the stomach drops.

arc.label: Bridge
arc.scene: The bravado thins out. For a second she just admits she has no clean way to feel about this.

lines.insight: The whole song folded into one image. Feelings as a moving car you cannot get out of.

lines.line: I hate you for what you did and I miss you like a little kid
lines.insight: Two opposite truths in one breath, and she refuses to pick one.

read.texture: Jangly, propulsive indie rock. The drums keep things moving even when the lyrics would rather lie down.
